clean_body: It has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution. Peter, If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. It didn’t stop when the FBI wanted to talk to me and other members of Congress. Because now the Department of Defense has announced that it is escalating its investigation into me. All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear. If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it, Peter. I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution. There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None. If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming. name: Gov. Andy Beshear
subject: Your support led the way
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
Month after month, I'm amazed at the support I see from this grassroots team.

Happy New Year, Peter! I hope you and your family were able to ring in the New Year with joy, love, and togetherness.

As we all get back into our routines and work schedules, I just wanted to send you this quick note. **Because of your incredible work, our team at In This Together PAC ended December successfully, reaching our monthly fundraising goal.**

Month after month, I'm amazed at the support I see from this grassroots team. You give your hard-earned dollars to our mission – helping us elect for-the-people leaders across the country. You read our emails and stay up-to-date on our work. And you sign our petitions, or take our surveys, and ensure your voice is a part of every decision we make.

This is the kind of support we need to win tough elections up and down the ballot in 2026. We're striving for a government that works for the people, that truly listens and supports the needs and concerns of the voters, not special interests. Sharing your time and input with our team is a critical part of making that happen.

I can't thank you enough for what you've done to support In This Together PAC in 2025, and I can't wait for what's to come this year.

Thank you,

Andy

body:
Thank you for being a part of this movement, and for investing in the leadership we all deserve.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122537258/592788049/-1585290222?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2026-01-02_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjc4NDQ3YjI0LWY5ZTctZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=j-1bVvmSGREJ-7G-my5CxjBZbWqOdYLkX6y9XSCxBKY=)
---
| Peter,

Thank you for being part of our Emerge community! Your support helped us to finish 2025 strong and set a solid foundation for our work in the year ahead. Looking forward, we have some serious victories to build on — and we’re thrilled that you’re a part of the team.

In 2025, Emerge women broke barriers and delivered historic wins across the country. Nearly 300 alums ran for office in 20 states, and 223 won — a 77 percent win rate. These alums are now ready to get to work, and so are we. As we prepare for the crucial midterms this November, 30+ Emerge alums are on the ballot for Congress who will help determine whether Democrats can flip Congress in November.

By championing leaders of the New American Majority – Black, Brown, and Indigenous women and women of color, as well as LGBTQ+, young, and unmarried women, who are part of the fabric of their communities, we’re prepared for breaking down barriers and paving a better path forward for our country.

But none of this work would be possible without you. Thank you for being a part of this movement, and for investing in the leadership we all deserve.

With gratitude,

Team Emerge

subject: RFK Jr was right again—are you ready to see why?
body:
For years, liberals called RFK Jr a quack... an alarmist... a conspiracy theorist.| | | [](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=c5e3ae095cf94013ac177c7981e157c7&_e=kBeZ2zceeJBY4lG2ybceYwSP_55LiFHkHM95peZPSd-Uj7Bj8-7QgmSONeoLF2vWckd97S-AwGx2Gx67dhw0y-tipuad8yCpoQg_Z2RMKbqehIk_0pmv4M27jc-WOHGeCOe39WBY4GqgJ_Y74gmRFRbpVKhBuyuzWoWtvgKx_9ZOmoFrUnUR8oQPf97-b3bsduNwdmaabbGOUWqa06_Sm28B9Q7rTXaco1BeWIZTW4wxXNR_4jCBxNS6TMBDmunEKkwAWlg_F1LyTQUtmDER6cbf-e5EReSkxC_Ve6d9PGywuU9Dt4_dvOLbeEmS0y4sc-Obesos49-DcJBILpgZJA%3D%3D)

---
January 02
---
| | |
---
| | **RFK Jr was right again—are you ready to see why?**
---
| [ Most Terrifying Truth ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=c5e3ae095cf94013ac177c7981e157c7&_e=kBeZ2zceeJBY4lG2ybceY6GajhaFjJjMOpuzb_DLPkdCsKdfdaOeYwtrG2RxMCG-9SnBl6ezlkbDQc9fMDtBnOYp1UC6PfdMIpuqW3M7pcSW_wHxauGCF3eIrsjGlw474D7DdYGTfEJERtxCQ0GYrdblzdIWJPg2YZ77UVN_t_A_8HFC11yxcmWjdTTux_ujq-0A4p1ZrRueWfPlHcYwWWlefuewdN9yiE4zFPBVU7BHwPPk8PCPiT7o__CXQzUSd5LTpUyrVxFWZwnTvjf-LYsyIcha5_put7JZExGIZNx5Ua1KdCvAyN8xZATeg-R4L6xjXYgUROQ5pmim2LEBoJvgnTKXsQnd1HRXdQzrmkKWv5aIBm_5Z1L4N_B7QdWP3ZU7MXd3CbkuQhTi-KyJilGXXljaviGncHYgicFAZO_xAZhZDP3IpRbRsHKI1B8mSVD9ZbPlxZcNRTkUzaFD3nA4C79r55WaEd5iE19YrCVn03WLAihfg1RI748nZqee1lFiKLclQytf7oVMRM9nWWckQeF8z7cVigeUCFGnSVwNOHRo_RxYBeM6Z_9Uz51uUq_jT50q1kSdsy9DSPlJow%3D%3D)
---
| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

But what if they were wrong?

He was **right** about:

* COVID lab origins
* Microplastics in our bodies
* FDA corruption

And now his new warning is the **most terrifying truth** Americans need to hear in 2026.

Millions are in the crosshairs...
If you were born before **January 1, 1965** , this threat affects you first.

[Get the facts yourself and learn why RFK Jr is sounding the alarm about a new emerging health crisis before it’s too late.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=c5e3ae095cf94013ac177c7981e157c7&_e=kBeZ2zceeJBY4lG2ybceY6GajhaFjJjMOpuzb_DLPkdCsKdfdaOeYwtrG2RxMCG-9SnBl6ezlkbDQc9fMDtBnOYp1UC6PfdMIpuqW3M7pcSW_wHxauGCF3eIrsjGlw474D7DdYGTfEJERtxCQ0GYrdblzdIWJPg2YZ77UVN_t_A_8HFC11yxcmWjdTTux_ujVOZemLe6rHh7LUsnwYNLR5eViSevk0JIF_U1_mgQjp_p7rMygpA1oLo8mgF-GjMtE_6npJtzIij9u3pzyKhzHj_5zBZn5yEykqlJjUFjstinE0INCFNJ188C9DKGR31aRubgYQ4f0fbXgD3lExp_kFkVHtbcs8lSfKFP6iuxoSx7DEMhVjwphVb8POILh2bO0zXcOWlKgowj1R7dsXsVGCpPYZXGPt6Q0EX_sgo0z3dRi_t9imvnpj9rU3Il87EXkv5o3m69o4j03Y9WR-x-VUcZV-aNhceJbtaYGWCE9kaWkUshikJHdr3LWTbcZwN5ArksqKmLDB71MZUnAYUKbORSZU1t_FgqJJTXQJGmdWVMHuSUEt1-CuxQ1HvVNskUwQBn-4hSY0cMlU4LHLs07w%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | A law Congress passed is starting to make its presence felt... | And that’s VERY bad news for retirees. Even the Wall Street Journal had to admit that the law... “Upends 20 Years of Retirement Planning and STICKS It to the Middle Class.” What does that mean for you? It’s time to find out... body:
| | | Hi there, it's Marc Elias, and last month I argued before the Supreme Court to defend our democracy.

[**Before I brief you further, I have to ask -- will you chip in $5 to the DSCC to stand against GOP leaders' flagrant disregard for the law and fight for our democracy by flipping the Senate blue? >>**](https://go.dscc.org/go/22350?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260102-MarcElias-MarcEliasDeliveredArgumentsBeforeSCOTUS-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260102-MarcElias-MarcEliasDeliveredArgumentsBeforeSCOTUS-Pros-sl1&akid=22194%2E9716520%2EYBm_un)

| | [Donate $5](https://go.dscc.org/go/22350?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260102-MarcElias-MarcEliasDeliveredArgumentsBeforeSCOTUS-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260102-MarcElias-MarcEliasDeliveredArgumentsBeforeSCOTUS-Pros-sl1&akid=22194%2E9716520%2EYBm_un)
---

In 2018, Donald Trump called me the Democratic Party's "best election stealing lawyer." But the truth is, I fight hard to ensure every eligible citizen can vote, every lawful ballot is counted, and every accurate election result is certified -- that's what Trump and his cronies are so afraid of and why I'm so _eagerly_ fighting them in court on behalf of Democrats.

In this case, the GOP is trying to override decades of precedent and undermine this country's campaign finance laws for their own benefit. They're trying to eliminate a key barrier to corruption so they can spend even more money from billionaires to prop up Republican candidates.

**But I'm fighting back, and together, we'll _win_.**

On behalf of the DSCC and Democrats like you across the nation, **I walked out of the Supreme Court where I personally delivered our arguments, urging the justices to _reject_ the GOP's _corrupt_ schemes.**

Now, we have to be prepared for any outcome. We MUST utilize every resource at our disposal to keep fighting back against Trump and his cronies, _no matter what_ \-- and that's where your support is critical.

The DSCC is the _only_ committee dedicated to electing a Democratic Senate to replace the GOP majority that rubber-stamps Trump's dangerous agenda. And the support of Democrats like you plays a key part in their work to preserve our democracy.

**So I'm asking for your help as we work to stop Trump's allies, flip the Senate blue, and defend democracy:**[**Please, will you chip in $5 or more today to support the DSCC's efforts to take back the Senate?](https://go.dscc.org/go/22350?amount=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260102-MarcElias-MarcEliasDeliveredArgumentsBeforeSCOTUS-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260102-MarcElias-MarcEliasDeliveredArgumentsBeforeSCOTUS-Pros-sl1&akid=22194%2E9716520%2EYBm_un)**

We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

## Resolutions in the new year...
Two days into 2026, I've been thinking about resolutions.
Most people make them with big ambitions, big plans for the new year.
But let's be honest, most resolutions are broken by the end of January, if not already--it can be hard to have the discipline to stick to whatever resolution you committed to:
Exercise more.
Eat healthy.
Sleep better.
They're all easier said than done.
And it got me thinking.
Those things I mentioned are all good goals, worth sticking to.
But what if this year, we focused more on getting our priorities straight?
I'm talking about spending time and energy on what really matters--faith, family, and country.
Just take a cue from the how the Trump administration tackled 2025.
They went into the year with a clear priority and a goal: put America first.
And they delivered.
A strong southern border, a booming economy, huge foreign policy wins. . . this administration didn't mess around.
It wasn't a lot of talk, lofty policy goals, or empty promises that never came true.
It was a crystal clear goal, made reality by a group that is laser-focused on restoring American greatness.
Trump committed to putting America first, and put together a team of high-achievers that could get the job done.
It's a good lesson in how to practically stick to your goals.
Have clear resolutions, based on what you want to prioritize in your life: your relationship with God, your family, and whatever else may be important to you.
Think about small steps to make each happen. Maybe it's 5 minutes of prayers every morning or evening. Each of these steps can turn into long term habits.
But if it stops there, it's likely to end up like so many resolutions committed to and then abandoned by February.
The key is surrounding yourself with like-minded people who can both support you and hold you accountable.
As ambitious as I feel going into a new year, I know I can't reach any of my goals alone.
That's why I keep good friends and family around me to help me get there.
So, as we head into 2026 take a second to pause and ask yourself what's most important to you.
What do you want to prioritize?
The answer--and the people you choose to have around you-- will help you keep those resolutions, into the new year and beyond.
. . .
As a final note, this year also marks the 250th birthday of the United States.
250 years of the greatest country on earth.
We've made it through a lot to get to this anniversary--I'm feeling optimistic about what 2026 has in store for America.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/eada8823-3cd0-4bdf-9a61-903f233fba66?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Cheers to 2026.

name: Powered by People
subject: It’s time to talk strategy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240575%2E444211%2Er8JpWT)
If flipping Texas is on your resolution list for 2026, then it’s time to talk strategy.
This year isn’t about one election. It’s a marathon. Texans could be voting up to **six times** in 2026—and our very first major opportunity to win is happening this month.
On January 31, we have a real shot to FLIP a State Senate seat in North Texas with Democrat Taylor Rehmet’s runoff election. Taylor is a veteran and union leader who nearly won outright in November, forcing a runoff after earning **48%** of the vote against two Republicans. This seat is within reach, but only if we organize early and show up strong.
Next comes March 3—the 2026 primary. This is where Texans choose the team that will carry us into November.
During that election, voters will decide our nominee for U.S. Senate, with the big contenders being Jasmine Crockett and James Talarico. We’ll choose who takes on Greg Abbott for Governor. And we’ll pick Democratic nominees for state legislative races across Texas. This is where the direction of our party is set before the biggest fight of the year.
Then comes the finale on November 3: the midterm election where we actually take seats and change who has power in Texas. _But here’s what can’t be ignored:_
Between those “Big Three” elections are special and local elections in April and May. These are the trap dates—low-turnout elections that fly under the radar, where momentum drops and Republicans often quietly lock in power.
**Powered by People exists to make sure that doesn’t happen again.**
Keeping track of every deadline, every runoff, and every election is practically a full-time job. That’s why Powered by People doesn’t just send a PDF or post once on social media. We give people a person, a guide they can rely on.
When you support Powered by People, you help connect Texans with a real, named volunteer who checks in, sends reminders, and makes sure no one misses an election, from January runoffs to the midterms in November.
**This is how Texas is won: by staying connected, building community, and showing up every single time.**
[**If you’re able, chip in today to help start our 2026 strong. Your contribution fuels year-round organizing, registering voters, training volunteers, and keeping Texans engaged through every election on the calendar.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260102.3W5Vhg&refcode=ema20260102-240575&t=3&akid=240575%2E444211%2Er8JpWT)
**[Donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260102.3W5Vhg&refcode=ema20260102-240575&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=240575%2E444211%2Er8JpWT)**
Happy New Year,
Powered by People

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20260102-240575-footer&t=23&refcode2=240575_444211_r8JpWT&akid=240575%2E444211%2Er8JpWT)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/update-contact-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=240575%2E444211%2Er8JpWT)

Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople.org](http://www.poweredxpeople.org?akid=240575%2E444211%2Er8JpWT), not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: Britney VanCitters
subject: Save the Date: Make Polluters Pay Advocacy Day
body:
Hi Peter,

Save the Date: OLCV and the Make Polluters Pay coalition are holding an Advocacy Day in Salem in February!

SAVE THE DATE: Make Polluters Pay Advocacy Day
When: Monday, February 16th, 2026 (all-day)
Where: Salem, OR

We will train you on how to talk with lawmakers about the Climate Resilience Superfund and share your personal story, then you and other community members will get to meet with lawmakers at the Capitol. This will be an all-day event in Salem.

Details and how to RSVP will be shared in the new year, but we wanted to save the date today.

Britney VanCitters (she/her)
OLCV Political & Organizing Director

P.S. If you want more practice connecting your story to your climate goals, join our January 27 Storytelling Workshop: RSVP here. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2gxiMZv_HUCVO3r0_Sx_1Q2]

https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv [https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv] https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org [https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org] https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F [https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F] https://x.com/OLCV [https://x.com/OLCV]

Oregon League of Conservation Voters
1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214
United States

Friend – I need to say thank you. I can’t tell you what it means to know that people like you have my back. I’m spending every single day working to do what I can to stop the Trump MAGA agenda. I’m fighting for healthcare funding for millions of Americans. I’m making every effort possible to stop GOP cuts that every day Delawareans and Americans rely on. _But I will also be honest: It’s an uphill battle with Trump in the White House and GOP control of Congress._ But that doesn’t mean the work we put in right now is for nothing, friend. If anything, it’s that much more important that we double down in our efforts and do everything we can to chip away at the cuts and destructiveness of this current Administration. My team is hoping to raise $5,500 from this email to build the momentum we need to take back the Senate in 2026. [**_Can you help us? (If everyone reading this gave just $3, we’d hit that goal in no time!)_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122539143/592795181/-489009331?amount=3&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.02_Gratitude_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_RS-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmN2Y3MjYzMy1mZGU3LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fCZh3Nd4mirVJpb9pctCxLDbRprOu92vZ9XnJDGsI68=) | | [**DONATE »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122539145/592795182/-489009331?amount=3&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.02_Gratitude_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_RS-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmN2Y3MjYzMy1mZGU3LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fCZh3Nd4mirVJpb9pctCxLDbRprOu92vZ9XnJDGsI68=)

These are tough times, friend.
But I don’t plan to back down from the fight. Do you?

Lisa
* * *

I’m making every effort possible to stop GOP cuts that every day Delawareans and Americans rely on. But I will also be honest: It’s an uphill battle with Trump in the White House and GOP control of Congress. But that doesn’t mean the work we put in right now is for nothing, friend. If anything, it’s that much more important that we double down in our efforts and do everything we can to chip away at the cuts and destructiveness of this current Administration. I couldn't have asked for a better community to fight with.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122539925/592798587/1328794144?refcode=ea.44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ0YmIwOWM1LWZkZTctZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=YCbNw-du8x1JpUrXxvqrWpHmV-BPMtd5VxDgjhMJm_8=&emci=35a285f5-30e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4823105)

Happy New Year, my dear friends! As we turn the page to 2026, I want you to know how deeply grateful I am to stand in this work alongside you. The past year tested our strength, our resilience, and our ability to find hope in the face of the unimaginable. And through every moment, you stood with this movement. **_Thank you._** Because of people like you, we helped elect more than 60 climate champions who are ready to protect their communities and challenge the power of the fossil fuel industry in places that have seen the very worst: asthma, cancers, diseases no one should have to learn the names of. _That’s the power of people coming together._ And now we begin a new year with clarity and purpose. 2026 will be a defining year — one where our climate champions will need every ounce of strength they can muster to flip the House and defend our democracy and our planet from forces determined to divide us. **[That work starts today. So I sincerely hope you’ll consider chipping in whatever you can today because we cannot do this work without you.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122539926/592798589/2105508926?refcode=ea.44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ0YmIwOWM1LWZkZTctZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=YCbNw-du8x1JpUrXxvqrWpHmV-BPMtd5VxDgjhMJm_8=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $5**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122539927/592798590/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ0YmIwOWM1LWZkZTctZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=YCbNw-du8x1JpUrXxvqrWpHmV-BPMtd5VxDgjhMJm_8=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122539931/592798592/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.44bb09c5-fde7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ0YmIwOWM1LWZkZTctZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=YCbNw-du8x1JpUrXxvqrWpHmV-BPMtd5VxDgjhMJm_8=)

clean_body: I couldn't have asked for a better community to fight with. | Happy New Year, my dear friends! As we turn the page to 2026, I want you to know how deeply grateful I am to stand in this work alongside you. The past year tested our strength, our resilience, and our ability to find hope in the face of the unimaginable. And through every moment, you stood with this movement. Thank you. Because of people like you, we helped elect more than 60 climate champions who are ready to protect their communities and challenge the power of the fossil fuel industry in places that have seen the very worst: asthma, cancers, diseases no one should have to learn the names of. That’s the power of people coming together. And now we begin a new year with clarity and purpose. 2026 will be a defining year — one where our climate champions will need every ounce of strength they can muster to flip the House and defend our democracy and our planet from forces determined to divide us. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = With appreciation, xx Jane = | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. name: Mark Kelly
subject: My decision
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
#

Hey Peter, it’s Mark Kelly.
Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate.
I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy.
But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something.
So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too.
I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help.
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.
After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do.
[**I’m asking you to split a donation between me and Ruben Gallego, and I hope you know that I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t important.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-mek-em-mar25.qIyu4X&refcode=em260102-1-160&t=1&akid=4054%2E734936%2EknMudH)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._
| | **[Donate $5](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1061?c0=ruben-mek-em-mar25.Savuis&refcode=em260102-1-160&t=20&akid=4054%2E734936%2EknMudH)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1046?c0=ruben-mek-em-mar25.Wa-Kh4&refcode=em260102-1-160&t=21&akid=4054%2E734936%2EknMudH)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1039?c0=ruben-mek-em-mar25.He4t6t&refcode=em260102-1-160&t=22&akid=4054%2E734936%2EknMudH)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1040?c0=ruben-mek-em-mar25.OI7bI2&refcode=em260102-1-160&t=23&akid=4054%2E734936%2EknMudH)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1041?c0=ruben-mek-em-mar25.SLSoL7&refcode=em260102-1-160&t=24&akid=4054%2E734936%2EknMudH)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1044?c0=ruben-mek-em-mar25.bSKvZs&refcode=em260102-1-160&t=25&akid=4054%2E734936%2EknMudH)**
---
****
Thank you,
Mark

name: Justice Democrats
subject: Zohran was just officially sworn in!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Hey Peter,

Yesterday, Zohran Mamdani was officially sworn in as Mayor of New York
City!

This is huge — not just for New York City, but for every working person
who’s been told we can’t beat the billionaires, corporate Super PACs, and
the political establishment.

Zohran’s win proved what our movement knows: when candidates run on bold,
working-class solutions — and refuse to cave when the heat turns up —
organized people can beat organized money.

But swearing-in day isn’t the finish line. It’s the starting point for the
rest of the country.

Now comes the part that scares the corporate establishment most: building
on this momentum so we can elect leaders like him to Congress nationwide.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260102.6N0GHT&refcode=em20260102-37336&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37336%2E1203867%2EmH22ms ]Will you chip in $5 today to help us build on Zohran’s
swearing-in momentum and fuel the next wave of people-powered wins in
Washington?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260102.6N0GHT&refcode=em20260102-37336&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1021&akid=37336%2E1203867%2EmH22ms ]Contribute $5

Every dollar helps us recruit and back working-class challengers, take on
entrenched corporate Democrats, and keep proving that politics can belong
to everyday people — not big donors.

Let’s make sure this wasn’t just a moment, but a movement.

In solidarity,

Team Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20260102-37336-footer&t=1022&refcode2=37336_1203867_mH22ms&akid=37336%2E1203867%2EmH22ms

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1023&akid=37336%2E1203867%2EmH22ms

body:
I wanted to send a quick note to say thank you for helping us reach our 2025 end-of-year fundraising goal!
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20260102-5773-footer&t=1&akid=5773%2E420207%2EP_0ruH)
Peter,
I wanted to send a quick note to say _thank you_ for helping us reach our 2025 end-of-year fundraising goal!
Last year was tough, with Donald Trump re-entering the White House and ICE terrorizing communities across the country. But this year, we have a big opportunity to take back the House and put a check on the MAGA agenda — and I’m counting on your grassroots support to help make it happen.
As one of the first members of Congress to reject corporate PAC money, I’m proud that our movement is powered by the same working people I’m fighting for every day in Congress.
So thank you, again, for everything you did to help us reach our goals last year. It’s making a big impact in what we’re able to do, from organizing nonviolent resistance all over the country to organizing to elect more progressive members of Congress in swing districts.

name: Mallory McMorrow
subject: Thanks so much
body:
[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/83?t=1001&akid=3482%2E898137%2ECbG0CY ][IMG]

Thank
you, team!

You helped us close out the last quarter of fundraising — and all of 2025
— in a strong position, and our whole team is so grateful to you.
Especially me!

I hope you had a restful holiday with your family and friends and are
ready to take on the new year.

Thanks to you, our campaign is in an even better place to take on national
Republicans’ money spigot spending machine, reach voters in every corner
of Michigan, and keep this Senate seat blue.

Here’s to all we’ll do in 2026, together.

Wishing you all the best for the new year,

— Mallory

name: Nancy Mace
subject: Alan Wilson owes Nancy Mace an apology
body:
Graham and Norman validate Mace's security threats as Wilson—who called her an "entitled, spoiled brat"—requests a taxpayer funded security detail | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/nancymace/alan-wilson-owes-nancy-mace-an-apology-10352920?e=ddc57b5ede)
---
| |
---
| | Friend, _**Today, Senator Lindsey Graham and Rep. Ralph Norman set the record straight on Rep. Mace and we say FINALLY, THANK YOU.**_
---
| [SUPPORT NANCY MACE](https://nancymace.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=9cf03b3006&e=ddc57b5ede "SUPPORT NANCY MACE")
---
| In _The State’s_ new report, both confirmed what I've been saying all along: _**"Nancy is right.**_ There's some pretty angry people out there." - Senator Lindsey Graham "We all get death threats, **_Nancy I think gets more probably than others._** " - Rep. Ralph Norman Let me be clear about what we're dealing with: **Over 1,300 death threats in 2025 alone.** In the wake of Charlie Kirk's assassination, political violence isn't slowing down, it's accelerating. And those of us who refuse to back down, who fight hardest for conservative values, we have targets on our backs. Senator Graham and Rep. Norman understand the reality my team and I face every single day. **REAL MEN protect women.** **Alan Wilson just requested his own taxpayer-funded security detail.** The same Alan Wilson who called me **_"entitled"_** and a _**“spoiled brat”**_ for asking for help for basic security to safely perform my duties while facing more than 1,300 death threats annually. Security is **_"entitlement"_** when it's a Conservative woman who calls it like she sees it, but perfectly justified when it's Alan Wilson's safety on the line? **Classic career politician who thinks his own rules don't apply to him.** I've been vindicated by Graham and Norman, and exposed Wilson's hypocrisy in the process. **I refuse to be silenced by the Left’s political violence and threats.** I refuse to quiet down to appease the violent Left. Thank you for standing with me through the attacks, the threats, and the cheap shots from career politicians like Alan Wilson. Your support means everything, and it's what allows us to keep fighting. **South Carolina First. Always.** Sincerely,
Nancy Mace
---
| [SUPPORT NANCY MACE](https://nancymace.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f826f615915b06bdee33acac8&id=d559c819f7&e=ddc57b5ede "SUPPORT NANCY MACE")
---

name: Our Revolution HQ via Our Revolution
subject: Here's how we're fighting back in 2026
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34909?t=1011&refcode=email-20250102_v&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

The American people are confronting an authoritarian onslaught with no
precedent in our nation’s history — and no guarantee that our democracy
will survive in any meaningful form if we fail to meet this moment.

Donald Trump and the oligarchs backing him are prosecuting and threatening
political opponents, unleashing ICE raids that tear families and
communities apart, deploying militarized force to intimidate dissent,
attacking the press and universities, and undermining the right to vote
through gerrymandering and voter suppression. They are stripping
healthcare, food assistance, and basic protections from working people —
all to fund massive tax breaks for billionaires and corporations.

We are the ones paying the price. It’s our friends and neighbors being
detained and disappeared by ICE. It’s our families losing healthcare as
Medicaid and the ACA are gutted. It’s our communities watching public
institutions hollowed out while corporate power tightens its grip.

This is not chaos. It’s a strategy — and it’s one we are organized to
defeat. With over 1 million members and a 57,000+ person Citizen Action
Team, we are deploying a coordinated, nationwide strategy to fight Trump
and the oligarchs on every front.

Our Revolution is America’s leading independent, grassroots-funded
political action organization. Founded by Bernie Sanders in 2016, we exist
to build power from the bottom up — and that mission has never been more
urgent.

A FEW HIGHLIGHTS OF OUR REVOLUTION’S WORK

We’ve been in close touch throughout this year so we won’t rehash
everything. But we do want to highlight some of where our time, energy,
and organizing power have been focused:

* Electing anti-oligarch champions — Our Revolution has now helped elect
more than 1,000 progressives at every level of government, from
Congress to city councils and school boards. In 2025 alone, our
organizing helped propel a new generation of bold leaders — including
victories like Zohran Mamdani in NYC — showing clearly where the
energy of the party lives.
* Building progressive governing power — We are helping build and defend
progressive majorities in cities like St. Louis, delivering wins on
housing justice, worker power, universal basic income, and direct
democracy.
* Mass mobilization and rapid response — Our volunteers have organized
thousands of local actions, town halls, protests, and pressure
campaigns targeting Trump and the oligarchs directly (at his golf
clubs, outside their Hamptons homes, and more). We’re demanding an end
to the cruelty and corruption, calling for immigrant rights and
economic justice, and pushing back against Trump’s authoritarian
agenda.
* Inside–outside party transformation — Our Revolution continues to be
one of the only organizations working inside the Democratic Party to
remove corporate influence, build progressive caucuses in 20 state
parties, and elevate grassroots leaders into decision-making roles.
We’ve moved party leaders to reject dark and corporate money in the
2028 presidential primary — now state and local leaders should ban it
from primaries in the 2026 midterms as well.
* Medicare for All and economic justice organizing — We’ve mobilized
millions of voters around universal healthcare, fighting back against
Medicaid cuts and privatization while advancing a clear, popular
alternative: healthcare as a human right. We’re also proud to stand
with workers like Starbucks and Amazon on the picket lines and in the
fight for a union.

All of this work adds up. Together, we are blocking some of the worst
abuses, electing leaders who refuse to bow to oligarch power, and building
the infrastructure needed to defeat Trumpism for good.

The truth is Trump’s cruelty is not abstract. It is lived every day by
working families, immigrants, seniors, and young people wondering if
democracy will survive. That’s why Our Revolution invests so heavily in
organizing people where they live — door to door, campus to campus, union
hall to union hall. Right now, our volunteers are organizing against ICE
raids and deportations, mobilizing mass protests nationwide, fighting to
Defund Oligarchs and Fund the People, and deploying our proven voter
turnout operation to win the midterms.

But so much depends on what we achieve in this coming year, and we have a
lot of work ahead of us.

OUR 2026 STRATEGY: FIGHTING ON EVERY FRONT

As we head into 2026, Our Revolution is focused on a targeted Three-Point
Strategy:

1. Fight Trump’s Oligarch Agenda — from Capitol Hill to City Halls. We
are organizing sustained pressure campaigns to save healthcare and
critical services, restore public media, and defund the oligarchs. Our
tax dollars should fund the people, not subsidize billionaires cashing
in on federal contracts while targeting working families and our
immigrant neighbors.
2. Elect Anti-Oligarch Champions Up and Down the Ballot. 2026 is a
make-or-break election year — and our chance to deflate Trump’s power
and take back control of the House and Senate. From Congress to
statehouses to local offices, we are recruiting, endorsing, and
electing candidates who refuse corporate PAC money and govern with
courage.
3. Get Oligarch Money Out of Politics and Uplift the Grassroots. We are
transforming the Democratic Party from the inside out — rooting out
corporate control and ensuring grassroots voices lead the way. Not
only are we making headway in the fight to get dark and corporate
money out of Democratic primaries, we’re building progressive power in
party leadership.

Trump pretends to understand working-class pain while selling out working
people to billionaires. We offer something real — and we’re not just
playing defense. Because as we stand up to Trump every step of the way, we
are also actively fighting for:

* Medicare for All
* Living wages and strong unions
* Climate action with good jobs
* Housing as a human right
* Immigrant justice
* A democracy free from corporate money

This agenda isn’t radical — it’s necessary. And it’s supported by
majorities of Americans across race, age, and geography.

Our Revolution — that’s you, our staff, our volunteers, and more than a
million grassroots supporters — is making a real difference. That’s why
MAGA fears us and Fox News is labeling us “Bernie’s Gestapo.”

Because when people move together, politicians notice. History shows that
sustained grassroots pressure is what forces change — and we are building
exactly that. But we are only able to do this work because people like you
choose to stand with us. If you can, please chip in today to support Our
Revolution’s work fighting Trump and the oligarchs:

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34909?t=1051&amount=22&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&refcode=email-20250102nd&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]Anything you can give — $5, $25, $50, $100, or more — helps us organize,
strategize and fight in 2026 and beyond.

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34909?t=1042&express_lane=true&amount=9&refcode=email-20250102nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]CHIP IN $9 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34909?t=1043&express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20250102nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]CHIP IN $22
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34909?t=1045&express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20250102nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]CHIP IN $27 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34909?t=1046&express_lane=true&amount=46&refcode=email-20250102nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]CHIP IN $46
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34909?t=1047&express_lane=true&amount=66&refcode=email-20250102nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]CHIP IN $66 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34909?t=1048&amount=9&refcode=email-20250102nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

If you’ve already donated, thank you. If now isn’t the right time, we
understand. Either way, we are grateful that you are part of this
movement.

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]Facebook | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10?t=1002&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]Twitter | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/11?t=1003&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]Instagram | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2355?t=1004&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]Youtube | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2356?t=1005&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]Website |

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/22227?t=1008&akid=10037%2E5885482%2EEFtkAk ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to be a 2026 Movement Builder

Our Revolution is America’s leading independent, grassroots-funded political action organization. Founded by Bernie Sanders in 2016, we exist to build power from the bottom up — and that mission has never been more urgent. A FEW HIGHLIGHTS OF OUR REVOLUTION’S WORK We’ve been in close touch throughout this year so we won’t rehash everything. But we do want to highlight some of where our time, energy, and organizing power have been focused: Electing anti-oligarch champions — Our Revolution has now helped elect more than 1,000 progressives at every level of government, from Congress to city councils and school boards. In 2025 alone, our organizing helped propel a new generation of bold leaders — including victories like Zohran Mamdani in NYC — showing clearly where the energy of the party lives. Building progressive governing power — We are helping build and defend progressive majorities in cities like St. Louis, delivering wins on housing justice, worker power, universal basic income, and direct democracy. Mass mobilization and rapid response — Our volunteers have organized thousands of local actions, town halls, protests, and pressure campaigns targeting Trump and the oligarchs directly (at his golf clubs, outside their Hamptons homes, and more). We’re demanding an end to the cruelty and corruption, calling for immigrant rights and economic justice, and pushing back against Trump’s authoritarian agenda. Inside–outside party transformation — Our Revolution continues to be one of the only organizations working inside the Democratic Party to remove corporate influence, build progressive caucuses in 20 state parties, and elevate grassroots leaders into decision-making roles. We’ve moved party leaders to reject dark and corporate money in the 2028 presidential primary — now state and local leaders should ban it from primaries in the 2026 midterms as well. Medicare for All and economic justice organizing — We’ve mobilized millions of voters around universal healthcare, fighting back against Medicaid cuts and privatization while advancing a clear, popular alternative: healthcare as a human right. We’re also proud to stand with workers like Starbucks and Amazon on the picket lines and in the fight for a union. All of this work adds up. Together, we are blocking some of the worst abuses, electing leaders who refuse to bow to oligarch power, and building the infrastructure needed to defeat Trumpism for good. The truth is Trump’s cruelty is not abstract. It is lived every day by working families, immigrants, seniors, and young people wondering if democracy will survive. That’s why Our Revolution invests so heavily in organizing people where they live — door to door, campus to campus, union hall to union hall. Right now, our volunteers are organizing against ICE raids and deportations, mobilizing mass protests nationwide, fighting to Defund Oligarchs and Fund the People, and deploying our proven voter turnout operation to win the midterms. But so much depends on what we achieve in this coming year, and we have a lot of work ahead of us. OUR 2026 STRATEGY: FIGHTING ON EVERY FRONT As we head into 2026, Our Revolution is focused on a targeted Three-Point Strategy: 1. Fight Trump’s Oligarch Agenda — from Capitol Hill to City Halls. We are organizing sustained pressure campaigns to save healthcare and critical services, restore public media, and defund the oligarchs. Our tax dollars should fund the people, not subsidize billionaires cashing in on federal contracts while targeting working families and our immigrant neighbors. 2. Elect Anti-Oligarch Champions Up and Down the Ballot. 2026 is a make-or-break election year — and our chance to deflate Trump’s power and take back control of the House and Senate. From Congress to statehouses to local offices, we are recruiting, endorsing, and electing candidates who refuse corporate PAC money and govern with courage. 3. Get Oligarch Money Out of Politics and Uplift the Grassroots. We are transforming the Democratic Party from the inside out — rooting out corporate control and ensuring grassroots voices lead the way. Not only are we making headway in the fight to get dark and corporate money out of Democratic primaries, we’re building progressive power in party leadership. Trump pretends to understand working-class pain while selling out working people to billionaires. We offer something real — and we’re not just playing defense. Because as we stand up to Trump every step of the way, we are also actively fighting for: Medicare for All Living wages and strong unions Climate action with good jobs Housing as a human right Immigrant justice * A democracy free from corporate money This agenda isn’t radical — it’s necessary. And it’s supported by majorities of Americans across race, age, and geography. Our Revolution — that’s you, our staff, our volunteers, and more than a million grassroots supporters — is making a real difference. That’s why MAGA fears us and Fox News is labeling us “Bernie’s Gestapo.” Because when people move together, politicians notice. History shows that sustained grassroots pressure is what forces change — and we are building exactly that. But we are only able to do this work because people like you choose to stand with us. If you can, please chip in today to support Our Revolution’s work fighting Trump and the oligarchs: [ ]Anything you can give — $5, $25, $50, $100, or more — helps us organize, strategize and fight in 2026 and beyond. If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Our Revolution will process immediately. [ ]CHIP IN $9 [ ]CHIP IN $22 [ ]CHIP IN $27 [ ]CHIP IN $46 [ ]CHIP IN $66 [ ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT If you’ve already donated, thank you. If now isn’t the right time, we understand. Either way, we are grateful that you are part of this movement. When we organize, we win. Our Revolution [ ]Facebook | [ ]Twitter | [ ]Instagram | [ ]Youtube | [ ]Website | [ ]Sign Up [ ]Our membership swag Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive group in America. [ ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the establishment by committing to be a 2026 Movement Builder with a recurring donation of just $5 or more a month! name: Roy Cooper
subject: I left office a year ago
body:
Grateful to have you with me in this fight.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=245%2E124072%2EfhGkJl)
Peter, when I left the Governor’s Office last January, it was with a great sense of humility and gratitude. Getting the chance to serve the state I love so much for eight years was an amazing opportunity. I thought that would likely be the end of my time in elected office, but it kept nagging at me that I might still be needed. **I saw how important this Senate seat was for not only our state, but for our country, and it got me thinking about a verse from the Book of Luke:** _To whom much is given, much is required._ I have received so many blessings in my life, Peter. Every race I’ve ever won; every opportunity I’ve ever been given — it was all possible because of people who helped me, supported me, and put their trust in me. **If there is still more I can do to give back to this country that I love, I have to try.** I know that we can win this toss-up seat. I wouldn’t be running otherwise. But I also know this will be the toughest fight of my career. The GOP is pouring funds into propping up my opponent, and polls are tightening. _I am asking for your help again._ **[I believe that there is so much left for us to accomplish together, and if we flip this seat blue and take back the Senate for the American people, we can start to get to work. Please, donate any amount you can to stand with me.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3703?t=7&akid=245%2E124072%2EfhGkJl)** | **[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3703?t=8&akid=245%2E124072%2EfhGkJl)**
---
Gratefully,
Roy Cooper
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3703?t=9&akid=245%2E124072%2EfhGkJl)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

clean_body: Grateful to have you with me in this fight. | Peter, when I left the Governor’s Office last January, it was with a great sense of humility and gratitude. Getting the chance to serve the state I love so much for eight years was an amazing opportunity. I thought that would likely be the end of my time in elected office, but it kept nagging at me that I might still be needed. I saw how important this Senate seat was for not only our state, but for our country, and it got me thinking about a verse from the Book of Luke: To whom much is given, much is required. I have received so many blessings in my life, Peter. Every race I’ve ever won; every opportunity I’ve ever been given — it was all possible because of people who helped me, supported me, and put their trust in me. If there is still more I can do to give back to this country that I love, I have to try. I know that we can win this toss-up seat. I wouldn’t be running otherwise. But I also know this will be the toughest fight of my career. The GOP is pouring funds into propping up my opponent, and polls are tightening. I am asking for your help again. | Gratefully, Roy Cooper Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here. National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us. Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party. name: Sharice Davids
subject: A result you can be proud of
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
We're confident, but we can't become overconfident

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122544910/592808815/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTdlOTc0OC0wNmU4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NV8bZZpUbW4v40SnWy1ytLPQeYGVnzH1QEdM6O99brQ=) I owe you a huge thank you. My team and I are still tallying the exact numbers, but one thing is clear: it’s a result every single person who has played a role in this movement should be proud of. **And we’re going to need those results.** Because this gerrymandering battle is coming up quickly — ten days to be exact — and things are heating up. Some days, Kansas Republicans are feeling confident. Some days, they’re not. Some days, I’m even feeling a little worried myself. But today? After seeing so many supporters come through in our hour of need? I’m feeling confident — and I hope you are too. Because this is an outcome we can control. If we work as hard as we possibly can over these next ten days to get our message out, push back against the National GOP’s lies, and raise real, grassroots opposition to this gerrymander, I know these lawmakers will back off.**We can keep this district in Kansans’ hands, not DC’s.** Here’s the thing, though: we can’t celebrate yet. We’re confident, but we can’t become _overconfident._ At a moment like this, when we’re so close to the finish line, we can’t take any off days. We need to sprint through the finish line — and that’s why I need your help. **_[I know my team and I have been asking for your support a lot lately, and I’m eternally grateful to everyone who’s chipped in. But if there’s ever been a moment to double down, this is it. Please, chip in a few dollars to help us get the job done. Your donation goes so much further than you could ever imagine.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122544911/592808816/-538008740?refcode=email_010226_overconfident&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=35&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTdlOTc0OC0wNmU4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NV8bZZpUbW4v40SnWy1ytLPQeYGVnzH1QEdM6O99brQ=)_** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122544912/592808817/-538008740?refcode=email_010226_overconfident&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=5&express_lane=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTdlOTc0OC0wNmU4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NV8bZZpUbW4v40SnWy1ytLPQeYGVnzH1QEdM6O99brQ=)

name: Senator Sean McCann
subject: Truly. Thank you.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF SEAN MCCANN
body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122545141/592809439/510768709?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTIzMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWJmZDVmYi0wNmU4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j365HaG5mAYVx2xwk9QPK0PEAV-Y1EJr2nAFlN0l7ok=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122545145/592809443/819412648?amount=5&refcode=email_010226_ty&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTIzMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWJmZDVmYi0wNmU4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j365HaG5mAYVx2xwk9QPK0PEAV-Y1EJr2nAFlN0l7ok=) Sean here. I wanted to take a second before the busyness of the new year starts to slow down and earnestly say _thank you._ The outpouring of support for my campaign throughout MI-04 has been incredible. From Battle Creek to Holland to the shores of Lake Michigan and everywhere in between, people are stepping up to join this fight and demand better from their leaders. And we deserve a whole lot better than Bill Huizenga. People are hungry for leadership that will deliver real results on the issues our families care about the most. That means working to lower the cost of groceries, make health care more affordable, and fighting back when politicians like Bill Huizenga jack up our energy prices. I am working tirelessly in the Michigan Senate to make a difference in our community – and that’s exactly what I intend to do if I have the honor of being elected to serve as the next Congressman from MI-04. But we have a long road ahead of us to get there. We have momentum on our side and a clear path to victory, but we need to stay focused and diligent every step of the way if we’re going to build the kind of movement it will take to finally flip MI-04 blue. I know with incredible supporters like you by my side, we can get this done and fend off whatever tricks National Republican groups, Donald Trump, and Bill Huizenga have up their sleeves. I wouldn’t be here without your support, so again: **thank you.** That’s all from me today, but I hope your New Year is off to a great start. More soon, Sean P.S. My team has already set some seriously ambitious fundraising benchmarks for us to hit the ground running in 2026 and start the Election Year off strong. So I don’t mean to be annoying, but they’re being very insistent that I ask: **_[could you please chip in $5 to help us start the year?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122545148/592809447/819412648?amount=5&refcode=email_010226_ty&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTIzMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWJmZDVmYi0wNmU4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j365HaG5mAYVx2xwk9QPK0PEAV-Y1EJr2nAFlN0l7ok=)_** I’m not some self-funding billionaire, which means I’m counting on folks like you to keep our momentum going strong. | | [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122545151/592809450/819412648?amount=5&refcode=email_010226_ty&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTIzMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWJmZDVmYi0wNmU4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j365HaG5mAYVx2xwk9QPK0PEAV-Y1EJr2nAFlN0l7ok=)
